rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs JOSEPH GARZA & MARY GARZA¿s motion for appointment of receiver is denied. ¿Defensive rights¿ are too uncertain and contingent to constitute ¿property¿ within the meaning of the enforcement of judgment statutes and, even if they ¿defensive rights¿ constitute property, the seizure and sale of such ¿rights¿ violates the due process clause of the federal and California constitutions. Assuming without deciding that In re Mozer was correctly decided with regard to defensive appeal rights, that case is readily distinguishable. With appeal rights, there has already been a determination of liability and the right to appeal is statutory, not constitutionally based. Unless and until there has been a determination of liability, the right to defend a claim is too uncertain and contingent to permit enforcement efforts. (See, e.g., Javorek v. Superior Court(1976) 17 Cal. 3d 629 [insurer's obligations to indemnify and defend were so contingent and uncertain that they were not subject to garnishment or attachment]; In re Marriage of Williams (1985) 163 Cal.App.3d 753 [pension benefits are contingent and uncertain and cannot be garnished until they are due and payable]; First Central Coast Bank v. Cuesta Tit. Guarantee Co. (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 12 [escrow funds cannot be garnished to recover broker's commission until contingencies are lifted]; and Dawson v. Bank of America (1950) 100 Cal.App.2d 305 [contingent debt is not subject to garnishment]. Even if ¿defensive rights¿ are property under California enforcement of judgment statutes, due process forbids the taking of ¿defensive rights¿ pursuant to enforcement of judgment efforts. While the court could not find any authority directly on point, the discussion at pages 394 & 395 of National Union Fire Insurance Co. v. City Savings (3rd Cir. 1994) 28 F.3d 376 provides a useful framework for resolving this issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs JOSEPH GARZA & MARY GARZA¿s motion for assignment of rights, restraining order and turnover order is denied without prejudice to re-filing a more narrowly tailored motion which specifies with considerably greater precision the rights sought to be assigned, the conduct sought to be restrained and the property sought to be turned over. The motion is now so broad that it is unclear what is covered by it and thus any order resulting from the motion is likely to be too vague to be enforced. Becaue the motion is so non-specific, the court cannot evaluate whether granting any or all of the motion violates ACL¿s due process rights. = (220/HEK)
